description_long: This object is a vintage-style tabletop radio, characterized by a rounded, arching wooden body with a faux wood grain texture. The front features a large, dark display area, likely representing a speaker grille, flanked by two cylindrical knobs at the bottom corners. Below the display is a hexagonal panel with a red background and a series of white and yellow markings, simulating a frequency dial. The sides and back are smooth, with visible wood grain patterns and no additional features or openings. The base is flat and slightly protrudes, providing stability for placement on flat surfaces. The overall design evokes early 20th-century electronics, blending decorative and functional elements.
description: A vintage tabletop radio with a rounded wooden body, large front display, and two control knobs. Features a red frequency dial and wood grain texture.
description_view_0: Front view showing the large dark display, two control knobs, and a red frequency dial panel.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the smooth, curved wooden surface with visible grain and no controls.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the plain, arched wooden back with subtle grain patterns.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the curved wooden surface and a hint of the front knob.
